data.title: Switzerland December seasonally adjusted unemployment rate 2.6% vs 2.6% expected
data.description:
Key points:
  • Prior 2.6%

The Swiss jobless rate held steady in December, although the unadjusted rate did creep up to 2.8% - up from 2.6% previously.

data.title: Jiangsu Lopal Tech Revises Investment Plan for Indonesia Project
data.description:
Jiangsu Lopal Tech revised its investment plan for Indonesia's lithium iron phosphate cathode material project, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai Bourse.

The 12,000-ton capacity project will be split into 3,000-ton Phase I and 9,000-ton Phase II. The 3,000-ton Phase will be handled by PT LBM Energi Baru Indonesia, while the remaining 9,000-ton Phase II will be managed by a newly established subsidiary with a capital of 135 million yuan.

data.title: CBOT soybeans may retest resistance at $10.14-3/4
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean March contract (SH25) may retest resistance at $10.14-3/4 per bushel, a break above which could open the way towards $10.30-1/2.

The contract managed to stabilize around support at $9.89-1/4 and climbed above the nearest resistance at $10.02. The stabilization indicates a valid break above a falling trendline.

The break is likely to trigger a strong rise. A break below $9.89-1/4 could be followed by a drop into the $9.64 to $9.73-3/4 range.

On the daily chart, the contract looks sideways in a narrow range of $9.83 to $10.05-1/2. However, the bullish divergence on the daily RSI suggests limited downside.

With the market approaching $10.05-1/2 again, it is biased to climb towards the $10.23-1/2 to $10.41-1/2 range.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: Yogi Plans MOA Expansion for Business Diversification
data.description:
Yogi said it plans to amend its memorandum of association to expand its business activities, according to a Thursday filing with the BSE.

The proposed changes include manufacturing, trading, and dealing machinery, tools, and equipment, including components and accessories. The company also plans to enter the business of general order supply, commission agency, and trading in a broad range of goods, according to the filing.

data.title: Japan's Nikkei slumps, dragged down by tumble in Uniqlo owner
data.description:
Japan's Nikkei share average sank for a third straight session on Friday, dragged down by a tumble for Uniqlo store chain operator following a disappointing earnings report.

Investors were cautious ahead of a closely watched U.S. monthly payrolls report later in the day, which could provide crucial clues on the Federal Reserve's policy path. Wall Street was closed overnight to mark the passing of former President Jimmy Carter.

The Nikkei index dropped 1.05% to 39,190.40, accelerating losses into the closing bell, bringing its three-day decline to 2.23%. For the week, it sagged 1.77%.

Of the Nikkei's 225 components, 183 fell versus 41 that rose, with one ending flat.

The broader Topix slipped 0.8%.

Providing a bright spot, takeover target Seven & i Holdings , which operates the 7-Eleven convenience store chain, jumped 4.86% following a Bloomberg report that Apollo Global Management is considering investing as much as 1.5 trillion yen ($9.47 billion) in a management buyout.

Nvidia supplier Advantest also lent support, climbing 5.12%.

The bulk of the Nikkei's declines were due to a 6.53% plunge in Uniqlo-owner Fast Retailing , by far the most heavily weighted stock on the index.

Despite recent weakness, "the Nikkei is likely to stay robust, supported by a weak yen", said Norihiro Yamaguchi, senior Japan economist at Oxford Economics.

Domestic earnings season will move into higher gear later this month and "overall results are likely to stay solid", he added.

The yen was steady at around 158.39 per U.S. dollar - not far from 158.55, the weakest level since mid-July 2024 reached earlier this week.

A cheaper yen inflates the value of overseas revenues for Japan's many heavyweight exporters.

($1 = 158.39 yen)

data.title: Copper Set for Strong Weekly Gain
data.description:
Copper futures rose above $4.29 per pound on Friday and were poised to gain more than 6% for the week, marking the best weekly performance since May of last year, when copper prices surpassed the $5 mark.

The bullish sentiment was largely fueled by optimism that China, the world’s largest consumer of copper, will follow through on its recent commitments to ramp up monetary and fiscal stimulus to bolster economic growth.

Copper prices also found support from expectations that strong demand for electric vehicles and renewable energy technologies will drive higher consumption of the metal this year.

However, rising global trade tensions and a potential slowdown in manufacturing, driven by looming tariffs under incoming US President Donald Trump, kept investors cautious.

data.title: Nikkei Falls 1.0%; Auto and Pharma Stocks Lead Declines — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks ended lower, as auto and pharmaceutical stocks led declines amid growing concerns of higher borrowing costs. Otsuka Holdings dropped 4.2% and Toyota Motor lost 2.4%. Fast Retailing fell 6.5% after weaker 1Q profit from China cast doubt on its outlook. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield climbed 2.5 basis points to 1.195%, its highest level since May 2011. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.0% to 39190.40. Investors await U.S. jobs data due later in the day. USD/JPY is at 158.36, compared with 158.14 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0648 GMT - Japanese stocks ended lower, as auto and pharmaceutical stocks led declines amid growing concerns of higher borrowing costs. Otsuka Holdings dropped 4.2% and Toyota Motor lost 2.4%. Fast Retailing fell 6.5% after weaker 1Q profit from China cast doubt on its outlook. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield climbed 2.5 basis points to 1.195%, its highest level since May 2011. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.0% to 39190.40. Investors await U.S. jobs data due later in the day. USD/JPY is at 158.36, compared with 158.14 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0641 GMT - Tata Consultancy Services is likely benefiting from some tailwinds, HDFC Securities analysts say in a research report, as they maintain the stock's add rating. These tailwinds include the Indian IT services provider's strong total contract value of $10.2 billion, driven by big deal wins across various markets and industries, the analysts say. Management's outlook of calendar 2025 growth appears to be better than calendar 2024, supported by an increase in successful deployment of generative artificial intelligence engagements, the analysts add. The brokerage raises the stock's target price to INR4,590.00 from INR4,545.00, based on 28x estimated FY 2027 EPS, above five-year average of 27x. Shares are 6.1% higher at INR4,283.00. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0634 GMT - Amorepacific Corp. may report solid 4Q earnings backed by a narrower loss in China, Mirae Asset Securities analyst Bae Song-yi writes in a note. Bae expects the South Korean cosmetics maker to post an operating profit of KRW84 billion for the October-December quarter, quadrupling from a year earlier. Progress in business restructuring in China likely led the company to narrow operating losses from its Chinese operations to KRW20 billion in 4Q from KRW30 billion in 3Q and KRW40 billion in 2Q, she reckons. Mirae Asset raises its target for the stock by 14% to KRW160,000 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 0.7% higher at KRW116,200. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0545 GMT - Zydus Lifesciences' earnings are poised to rise on higher sales contribution from Sitagliptin 505b(2) drug, Nomura analysts say in a research report. Assuming Sitagliptin market at $300-$500 million at a discounted price, the opportunity size for Zydus Lifesciences could be as big as $100-$150 million annualized, the analysts say. The brokerage lifts its earnings estimates for the Indian pharmaceutical company by 1% for FY 2025, 6% for FY 2026, and 6% for FY 2027. It raises the stock's rating to buy from neutral and the target price to INR1,140.00 from INR1,030.00. Shares are 0.4% higher at INR1,013.10. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0532 GMT - Tencent's overall business fundamentals and growth outlook remain intact despite recently being added to a Pentagon list with alleged Chinese military ties, OCBC analysts write in a note. However, the news may indicate rising geopolitical tensions and could be a share-price overhang until further clarity on the development, they say. Tencent's key businesses are value-added services and online advertising, which remain positive over the long term. The recent share-price selloff may be an overreaction and provides for a long-term accumulation opportunity, they say. OCBC maintains a buy rating on the stock at fair value of HK$560.00. Shares are last at HK$373.00. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0506 GMT - New Zealand's S&P/NZX 50 closed 0.4% lower at 12895.98. The benchmark index is down 1.6% early in the new year, running on thin volumes and little stock-specific news during the Southern Hemisphere's summer holiday season. The index was weighed by losses in financial and telecom stocks in Friday's session. Westpac posted the biggest daily fall, shedding 2.1%. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Airbus's 2025 Outlook Will Largely Depend on Engine Deliveries — Market Talk
data.description: Airbus's year-end results are positive for its 2025 outlook, but engine deliveries are a key factor for the company's prospects this year, RBC Capital Markets' Ken Herbert says in a research note. The European plane maker delivered 123 aircraft in December to end the year with 766 shipments to customers, short of the 770 the company targeted but ahead of consensus expectations of 755. "We believe the key bottleneck to greater A320 [narrow-body jet] deliveries in 2024 was the engine supply chain, with the Leap in particular," the analyst says, referring to the engine made by the Safran-GE Aerospace joint venture CFM International. RBC expects Airbus to guide for around 825 deliveries in 2025, which would be positive for investor sentiment, the analyst says. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

data.title: GCL New Energy to Acquire 100% Stake in Investment Holding Company
data.description:
GCL New Energy agreed to acquire the entire issued share capital of Harmonic Century Global from Golden Concord Group for 325 million yuan, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Of the total consideration, 200 million yuan will be paid in cash. In comparison, 65 million yuan will be settled by way of allotment and issue of 153.4 million consideration shares at HK$0.45 apiece by the company.

The company will settle the remaining 60 million yuan by way of the issue of convertible bonds, and upon completion, the target company will become a subsidiary of the company.

GCL Petroleum and Natural Gas, a subsidiary of the target company, will continue to provide certain loans to Jiangsu Xiexin Electric Power and Taicang Harbour Golden Concord Electric-power Generation after the completion of the acquisition.

These tailwinds include the Indian IT services provider's strong total contract value of $10.2 billion, driven by big deal wins across various markets and industries, the analysts say. data.title: Thai Central Bank Unlikely to Cut Again Until April — Market Talk
data.description: The Bank of Thailand is in no rush to cut again, Nomura economists say, expecting it to hold rates steady until April. "The BOT has emphasized the need to preserve policy space amid heightened uncertainty," Charnon Boonnuch and Euben Paracuelles write in a note. They expect it will keep the policy rate steady at 2.25% in February, before delivering 25bp cuts in April and August, when more evidence of economic weakness will have emerged. Softer global growth, trade protectionism and slow tourism recovery pose downside risks to the Thai economy, they add. Domestically, renewed political uncertainty could result in weak budget execution. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: China Hongqiao Files for Singapore Listing of $330 Million of Bonds
data.description:
Aluminum producer China Hongqiao filed for the listing of $330 million worth of 7.05% senior bonds due 2028, on the Singapore bourse, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Friday.

The bonds will be listed and quoted in the Bonds Market on Jan. 13, the filing added.

data.title: S&P Assigns BBB- Credit Rating with Stable Outlook to Stoneweg European REIT
data.description:
S&P Global Ratings assigned an investment grade BBB- long-term issuer credit rating with a stable outlook to Stoneweg European Real Estate Investment Trust , formerly Cromwell European Real Estate Investment Trust.

The outlook reflects the trust's "sound portfolio quality" and "cash flow visibility" over the next couple of years, the rating agency said.

The rating agency added that it expects the REIT to manage its financial leverage sensibly during asset enhancement or redevelopment work.

The REIT has 105 assets in 10 European countries as of 2024-end.

data.title: In month since change of guard at central bank, Indian rupee's volatility picks up
data.description:
The Indian rupee's volatility picked up in the one month following the change of guard at the Reserve Bank of India, and analysts reckon that the central bank will be taking a more flexible approach to managing the exchange rate.

Since career bureaucrat Sanjay Malhotra took charge as the RBI chief on Dec. 10, replacing Shaktikanta Das, the rupee has declined by more than 1% against the dollar and gauges that measure its volatility have nudged higher.

The dollar-rupee pair's 1-month daily realized volatility touched a near-six-month high late in December while implied volatility (INR1MRR=), a gauge of future expectations, rose to 3.5%, its highest since August 2023.

The 1-month realized volatility had sunk to an over-20-year low in the middle of 2024.

That tranquil run is unlikely to be repeated, analysts said, with the central bank's capacity to intervene in the forex markets challenged by multiple headwinds.

"The RBI has demonstrated higher tolerance for a weaker INR in the last few weeks. We think the RBI will now use its ammunition more judiciously given the high level of uncertainty," Standard Chartered Bank said in a note on Friday, lowering its rupee forecast to 86.25 by March 2025 from 84.50 earlier.

ANZ Bank is more pessimistic, calling for the rupee to decline to 88 by March, while pointing out that "greater flexibility (for USD/INR) is inevitable".

The calls for higher flexibility and a weaker rupee come amid India's foreign exchange reserves declining over $64 billion from their record high in September on persistent dollar-selling interventions.

The rupee was slightly weaker at 85.88 against the U.S. dollar on Friday, after slipping to an all-time low of 85.9325 in the previous session.

Traders are already sensing the change with many pointing to a "tactical shift" in the way the RBI intervenes.

While the central bank "stubbornly defended levels before, it seems more focused on preventing runaway moves now while allowing wider intraday ranges," a senior trader at a state-run bank said.

data.title: Market Chatter: New Investors Join Class Action vs Switzerland Over Credit Suisse Debt Wipeout
data.description:
Switzerland is facing more investors in a lawsuit related to the government-orchestrated wipeout of 17 billion euros of Credit Suisse's debt, London's Financial Times reported Friday, citing an amendment complaint filed Wednesday in New York.

The total value of the claims increased to over $370 million with the addition of 39 plaintiffs to the case. There were only eight claimants when the case was initially filed in June 2024 and the claim value was $82 million.

The investors claim the government "folded to virtually all" of UBS Group's demands, including the wipeout of Credit Suisse's AT1 bonds, in rescue takeover of its former rival.

FT, citing a person familiar with the matter, said the government is expected to file a new motion for the dismissal of the claim in the coming weeks.

UBS and the Swiss government did not immediately respond to requests for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

Order book TCV stood at $10.2 billion, up 19% q-o-q/25% y-o-y. Book-to-Bill ratio stood at 1.3x. data.title: Puregold Price Club Refutes Report on PHP 137.8 Billion Revenue for 2024
data.description:
Puregold Price Club refuted a report on the Manila Standard website stating that it logged 137.8 billion Philippine pesos in gross revenues in 2024, according to a Friday Philippine bourse filing.

The consumer goods trader, however, confirmed that its consolidated net income was 6.9 billion pesos in the first nine months of 2024, as well as its same-store sales growth as these were previously disclosed to the Philippine Stock Exchange.

data.title: Holcim Names Chairman Jan Jenisch as CEO of Planned North American Spinoff
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

Holcim appointed Jan Jenisch as the chief executive of its planned North American business spinoff and proposed Kim Fausing to succeed him in his current role as chairman.

Jenisch is set to take the helm of Holcim's planned North American business after the execution of the spinoff, expected by the end of the first half, the Swiss building materials company said Friday. The spinoff is set to have a 10-member board.

Jenisch will remain Holcim's chairman until the annual general meeting on May 14, the company said.

He previously served as Holcim's CEO from 2017 to 2024.

The company added that it has proposed Kim Fausing to take on the seat of chairman left open by Jenisch. Fausing's appointment as chairman of Holcim is subject to a vote at the company's upcoming annual general meeting.

Kim Fausing has served on Holcim's board of directors since 2020 and he has been the president and CEO of Denmark's Danfoss Group since July 2017.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Nousouken Sees FY Net Y75.00M
Nousouken 1Q Parent Net Y70.00M Vs Net Y29.00M
Stocks fall, US yields near 8-month high ahead of jobs data
data.description:
Key points:
  • U.S. stock futures fall after trading closed overnight
  • Nikkei down 0.9%, Chinese bond yields climb
  • U.S. payrolls data critical to bond market
  • Dollar towers near 2-yr peaks, up for 6th straight week

Global share markets were under pressure on Friday as investors counted down to a U.S. jobs report later in the day that could exacerbate or ease the sell-off in the global bond market, while the dollar stood near two-year highs.

Both Nasdaq futures and S&P 500 futures were down 0.3%. Wall Street was closed overnight to mark the funeral of former U.S. President Jimmy Carter.

Pan-European STOXX 50 futures and UK FTSE futures were flat.

The closely watched U.S. nonfarm payrolls report is due at 0830 U.S. Eastern time (1330 GMT). Median forecasts are for a rise of 160,000 in jobs in December with unemployment holding at 4.2%.

Anything stronger could see 10-year Treasury yields spike to 13-month peaks and lift the U.S. dollar in the process.

Analysts at ING believe a print below 150,000 jobs would be needed to stop Treasury yields from rising further.

"Payrolls, as always, are a pivotal report. But we need to deviate materially from consensus to have an effect this time around," said Padhraic Garvey, regional head of research, Americas, at ING.

"Given the move already in Treasuries, there is some talk that Friday's numbers will need to be strong to continue this momentum, and in that sense there is some vulnerability for a lower yield reaction to a consensus outcome."

In Asia, Japan's Nikkei fell 0.9%, taking its weekly loss to 1.6%. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) was off 0.5% and headed for a weekly decline of 1.2%.

China's blue-chips slipped 0.4% and Hong Kong's Hang Seng dropped 0.5%.

Chinese government bond yields climbed after the central bank said it has decided to suspend treasury bond purchases temporarily due to short supply of the bonds.

Overnight, Philadelphia Fed President Patrick Harker said he expects the U.S. central bank to cut interest rates, but added that an imminent move down isn't needed. Kansas City Fed President Jeff Schmid signaled a reluctance to cut interest rates.

Markets have already scaled back expectations to around 43 basis points of U.S. rate cuts for 2025, while concerns about President-elect Donald Trump's potentially inflationary agenda have helped drive up longer-term yields.

The benchmark 10-year U.S. Treasury yield climbed 1.5 basis points to at 4.6957%, just below an eight-month peak of 4.73% hit on Wednesday. The big chart level is 4.739% and if that breaks, bears would be targeting the psychologically important level of 5%, which has not been seen since 2007.

The climb in Treasury yields - up about 9 bps this week - has bolstered the dollar index to 109.30, gaining for a sixth straight week.

Worries about Britain's economy have kept the pound under pressure and hit gilts especially hard, driving yields to 16-1/2-year highs, although they have retreated somewhat (GB10YT=RR).

The pound slipped 0.2% on Friday to $1.2278, having touched its lowest since November 2023 overnight. It is down 1.1% this week.

Oil prices rose on Friday. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude futures rose 0.5% to $74.32 and were set for a weekly gain of 0.5%.

Gold prices rose 1.3% in the week to $2,674.44 an ounce, near its highest level since December.

data.title: Megawide Construction to Fully Own Megawide GMR Construction JV
data.description:
Megawide Construction approved its chief executive officer, Edgar B. Saavedra, and Treasurer, Christopher A. Nadayag, to conclude a deal with GMR Global.

Under the deal, the company will buy GMR Global shares in their joint venture, Megawide GMR Construction JV, according to a Thursday Philippine bourse filing.

Following this acquisition, the joint venture will now be fully owned and controlled by Megawide, the construction company noted.

data.title: Manufacturing Boosts Malaysia's Industrial Production by 3.6% in November
data.description:
Malaysia's Industrial Production Index (IPI) rose 3.6% year-on-year in November 2024, marking the eleventh straight month of growth, government data showed Friday.

The manufacturing sector led with a 4.6% increase, up from 3.3% in October, while electricity output grew 3.9%. Mining output fell 0.8%, an improvement from October's 2.8% decline.

Export-driven industries surged 5.6%, with electronics, petroleum products, and plastics leading the way.

Domestic industries grew 2.6%, driven by food processing, fabricated metal products, and non-metallic mineral products. On a monthly basis, IPI increased by 0.5%, following a 1.7% rise in October.

This positive data comes after Malaysia's central bank kept its Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) at 3%, unchanged since May 2023, as it continues to prioritize economic growth amid global uncertainties.

From January 2024 to November 2024, Malaysia's IPI grew by 3.7%, supported by gains in mining, manufacturing, and electricity.

Despite global industrial production growth in China, Singapore, and Vietnam, Malaysia's manufacturing sector outperformed expectations, while Japan, the US, and Thailand saw declines.
